reviews.title: Great Customer Still Exists
reviews.body:
Great Customer Still Exists
I haven’t visited the West Bromwich Building Society in years, but as part of switching mortgage lenders I needed to provide three months of bank statements.

I wanted to say how impressed I was with the service I received. *Personal information removed by Feefo* – and her colleague (apologies, I didn’t catch her name) – were absolutely lovely. They handled everything swiftly and professionally, and *Personal information removed by Feefo* even suggested I could come back and set up the transfer in readiness. And thought let's do this now! Had she not highlighted this, I would have needed to make another trip, so she really saved me time.

Please pass on my sincere thanks to them both. It’s very rare these days to experience such great customer service. 👌🏿

reviews.title: Poor service
reviews.body:
Poor service
Very poor. No-one was able to answer my query regarding transferring money from one account to another!!  Something as basic as this was not taught to staff. It seems you have to close the existing account, transfer money out into your Bank, then open a new account as if you're a new customer even though I have had money in West Bromwich for 10 years. So I would have to start all over again, as if I am a new unknown customer even though they already have all my details! Not possible to move money across from one account to another within the same building society.
This building society needs to move into the 21st century.

reviews.title: Obliging  Team
reviews.body:
Obliging  Team
Welcoming, helpful and informative  team.
As im sure you are aware you can often hear  other people's  conversations.On this occasion there was a lady who was being a little challenging! The team member who was dealing with this lady apologised for it taking her a little  while.
Another member of staff intervened to explain that the team  member was a new member of staff and asked the customer  if she could just  bear with  them a little while so that they could complete the  transaction  as quickly as possible.  The customer  continued  to huff and puff but the staff remained  calm cool and collected which was lovely to see..
I have never experienced any problems in my many years of being a customer whilst  dealing with any staff member.

reviews.title: Great service — welcoming, polite, and efficient team.
reviews.body:
Great service — welcoming, polite, and efficient team.

reviews.title: Opening a savings account
reviews.body:
Opening a savings account
Went into Oswestry branch to open savings account. 

Cashier advised we would need to make an appointment. 

An appointment was made the next day at 11am which happened to be Remembrance Day. 

On arrival for our appointment, we were 5minutes early we were greeted and asked to wait. 

I noticed that no one at the branch had realised the 2 minutes silence was to take place until I mentioned it. A customer came in and was served by the cashier during the 2  minutes silence which I thought was disrespectful.

We (my husband and I)were greeted by Elle-May and taken into a private room to open a savings account.

Elle-May was very friendly and professional taking through the savings account T&C and she made us feel very relaxed. 

The process was very quick and she was very knowledgeable, and was able to answer all our questions.

I would highly recommend West Brom Building Society Oswestry Branch for excellent customer service and advise to anyone who wishes to open a savings account.

reviews.title: Beware online savings portal - even worse after recent changes
reviews.body:
Beware online savings portal - even worse after recent changes
I save with the West Brom because they offer good interest rates on certain savings accounts. I don't live locally, so I have an online account. When you contact the West Brom personally the staff are courteous and helpful. But this review is about the online savings portal. When I first became a member the portal was rudimentary, but just about functional. Recent changes mean you now can't even get a basic transaction listing, which you can print out, anymore. Now you get only the last 3 or 4 transactions in a pop-up. In addition I've just discovered that if you switch to a different account your login changes!? I moved from one limited access account to another and as part of the account application I asked West Brom to move the funds over and close the old account. Tried to log in with my existing details and they have all re-set, apparently because the account number is effectively the user name when logging into the savings portal. Hence none of my existing log-in details work. No idea what happened when you have multiple savings accounts. Do you need a different set of log-in details to look at each of those accounts? 
The savings portal only gives you the bare minimum, with none of the Account Details page, which would show account holders (joint accounts are possible), or ways to change your nominated account etc. Standard stuff that's normal on other Building Societies' web access for savings accounts is just not there. 
Completely mystifying is the fact that despite not having a facility to print out transaction listings online you don't even get an annual statement sent to you (digital or in the post).

reviews.title: Hearing difficulties in branches.
reviews.body:
Hearing difficulties in branches.
Everything about my experience with you was excellent except for one small point. 
My business was transacted speedily and to my complete satisfaction. It was also a help to have your opening hours displayed on your website. That saved me a wasted journey which was appreciated. 
The one area which I'm afraid I have to say gave me significant problems was not being able to make out what your cashier was saying.
I am quite hard of hearing and while I appreciate the need for security to be provided by thick glass partitions between your staff and customers you will understand that this can make it very difficult to keep  up with what your staff are saying.
Also when we lose hearing the usual thing that happens is that the higher frequencies are lost first. Since female voices are pitched higher than male, the fact that most cashier staff are female adds to the problem since their voices are at a higher frequency.
In saying this, I speak for significant numbers of your customers. The number of people in the U.K. with hearing loss is one in seven, so many of the customers who come in and use your services will have the same problems.
I'm sure you don't need me to tell you that there are electronic aids such as microphones and speakers which can be fitted to help deal with this. However, such aids tend to fail and need repair. Perhaps the simplest and cheapest and most reliable way to meet the needs of this section of your customer base would be to erect notices inviting customers to say if they are having trouble hearing and would they like to talk to a cashier in an office where they would be..........                           
 a)  Face to face with the cashier and therefore able to lip-read, without a thick glass barrier intervening.
 b)  There would be no background noise behind a closed door.
I'm sure your cashiers would have enough insight to know which customers could be trusted to be alone with them for a few minutes in an office.
Thank you.

reviews.title: Grabbing Victory from the Jaws of Defeat
reviews.body:
Grabbing Victory from the Jaws of Defeat
I have been saving with West Brom BS for circa 20 years. Intodays financial environment savings interest is important. 
In mid October 25 I went into my local WBBS and asked a) what were my savings accruing, and b) what products were available to improve returns. The response provided was i) poor, and ii) fortunately incorrect.
Over the subsequent few days I decided to move my WB BS savings to a competiting organisation, paying x 2 WB BS. I returned to WB BS at the end of October and advised of my plan. I asked again before starting the lanned action whether a competiting product existed, where upon the Customer Services representative stated yes. I have now opened a reasonably competitive account, and transferred funds.

The good action of the WB BS team managed to grap victory from the jaws of defeat eventually.

reviews.title: Good but more work needed on IT side
reviews.body:
Good but more work needed on IT side
The mortgage application process was smooth and straightforward, with everything handled efficiently and without unnecessary delays.

The only downside is the online account management. The mortgage account number isn’t provided in a digital format, and there’s no option to create an online account for easier access. As a result, you need to keep the account number written down somewhere, which feels like a security concern, especially since account access relies on easily discoverable personal information (address and date of birth).

Overall, the experience was positive, but improving the digital experience and account security would make it excellent.

reviews.title: Puts the "customer" into customer service
reviews.body:
Puts the "customer" into customer service
Layla put the customer as the focus of her service. She was friendly and polite and listened to my queries. Not only did she answer them in a simple and clear way, she also gave me possible reasons the receiving bank could be struggling with the transfer.  When I did contact them it turns out she was right. She also clarified, without me having to ask, what next steps should be and what would happen to my websave online account when my isa was closed.  I'm leaving for a better return on my savings, but am sorry to leave your excellent customer service behind.  I opened a new savings account earlier this year with you based on past experience of how you treat customers, and should your isa rates improve i will transfer back, again for the excellent service.

reviews.title: Good Staff, not so good Admin.
reviews.body:
Good Staff, not so good Admin.
We feel that notification of maturing investments is too late.  
The undertaking in the documentation is 14 days before maturity date, but that has never happened for us.  When we query this, the only reply we get is that there is a 21 day leeway after maturity for "change of mind".  This is really not the point.  It is messing about that is entirely avoidable  and has caused us some difficulty.  
Also, the interest rates lately are not attractive and consequently we are planning to move money elsewhere (unless there is an improvement).

reviews.title: Not the same - it wasn't the same!
reviews.body:
Not the same - it wasn't the same!
My experience with them almost exactly a year ago was very different. Trying to obtain a Mortgage agreement in principle took one phone call last year, simple process, friendly and helpful - this year took an age on the phone, a promise to return a call which never happened - no information or confirmation via email of my application and I had to chase them for an answer. I suspect had I not chased - I would still be waiting for the call. I think whatever the reason - the experience lacked the same warmth that prompted me to contact them in the first place.

reviews.title: Zoe could not have been more helpful
reviews.body:
Zoe could not have been more helpful
Exemplary.  My query regarded the setting up of a new account and the transfer in of an existing account.  Zoe was friendly, clear and helpful.  She needed to check with her colleagues in the ISA team, who had left for the day, and promised to call me back the following morning at 9:30.  I received the call at exactly 9:30, and Zoe was able to resolve my query over the phone.  First class customer service.

reviews.title: Happy
reviews.body:
Happy
My visit to the west Bromwich building society. Concerned Correspondence received. Information regarding a decrease on my interest rate on my isa ..I enquired about my options regarding my account with a gentleman named Steve. Who was extremely informative and polite. I was impressed. I thought i would have to book an appointment with the manager.  It was not the case fortunately. Steve arranged within 5 minutes. A Relocated of my investment. To a fixed rate isa for 12 months. Obviously the interest rate was higher than I was receiving previously..much appreciated on my behalf  I will just have to wait now .to see if Steve gave me the correct advice. Revisit in 12 months hopefully 🙏 Steve might be on duty?? ..l

reviews.title: Concerning lack of communication within the society, slow to act on instruc
reviews.body:
Concerning lack of communication within the society, slow to act on instruc
I am basing this rating purely on my recent experience, as I have not had disappointing service before. My ISA matured on 31/08/25 and yet my maturity documents were not received until the 23rd, a bank holiday weekend.
Despite calling in to a branch on the next day when it was open (Tuesday the 26th), in order to give my instructions as the disposal of my maturing balance, I have still not received monies into my bank account. I spoke at length yesterday to a friendly and very helpful lady named Annette who has messaged the ISA team with the gist of our conversation. I hope to receive the money tomorrow, or I shall have to complain to the society, as the funds were earmarked for a large purchase this week.
I have always preferred to deal direct with building societies rather than make online transactions as I had thought that these sort of problems would not occur with face to face dealings.

reviews.title: Disappointing service
reviews.body:
Disappointing service
Took a long time to get through on the phone and in total with the adviser, the call took 45 mins. To be fair there was the option for them to ring me back which I declined.
Once on the call, the adviser said the ISA transfer had gone through early rather than at maturity as I had requested, as it was an easy access cash ISA, although it's called double access.  As a result I had lost interest on the account, so I said I wanted to raise a complaint.  She offered me £20 on the condition that she close my complaint there and then.  If I didn't accept I was told I would have to go through the financial ombudsman. The £20 will be issued as a cheque!

reviews.title: Advisor excellent, Websave, not so much
reviews.body:
Advisor excellent, Websave, not so much
Service I received was as good as could be expected. The advisor was friendly, efficient, professional and as helpful as possible. 

However the Websave system was none of the above. 

I wanted to change my nominated bank account, online, but this proved impossible (I think because it is a joint account). 

The only way to achieve this it seems is for a form to be sent to me (by snail mail) which myself and my wife must complete and sign, then attach a recent original paper statement for the account I want to designate, before returning it. (Like most of the population I no longer have paper statements, so have had to request one.)

My wife and I could not do this even in a in branch, it seems, because the Branch system and the "Websave" system are totally separate.

I did understood that a Websave account was an online account, but I did expect to be able to do any administration necessary ONLINE.

reviews.title: please listen to the customer's question
reviews.body:
please listen to the customer's question
I wanted to ask a question about a maturing account, the answer was 'you've phoned too early, you need to wait another week as we send the information 14 days before the maturity date'.
Simple?
Unfortunately due to the poor quality of your recorded menu options I wasn't sure if I was talking to the correct person. I explained I couldn't hear very well, apologised if I was in the wrong section and asked my question about my maturing account.
The member of staff suggested that he could make a note on my account to say I couldn't hear very well, to save me explaining each phonecall, a very good idea.... except he had to read out the privacy terms and conditions for adding the note to my account.  
"sorry, what did you say? I can't hear very well" "you need to agree to the privacy terms and conditions for me to add the note" sorry, can you speak up please, never mind, yes to whatever you just said, can you tell me about my maturing account please?" "is there any way we can help you with your hearing impairment when you phone us?" "yes you could help me by explaining about my maturing account please" "do you wear hearing aids?" "yes, can you tell me about my maturing account please" "is it ok to add that to your account?" "that'll be lovely, thanks, can you....." "I'll just need to read out the privacy terms and conditions for that..... etc etc etc

reviews.title: Fab service, very welcoming
reviews.body:
Fab service, very welcoming
The lady I dealt with took her time to explain to me all I needed to know, and then help me put my request into action. Very happy with the support.
On placing my request via your portal, I was sent an SMS text message stating: 
"Thank you, we have now received your request. You will receive another text message once your request has been actioned."
Well, I never did receive another text message and it was only on checking my email days later, that I noticed you trying to contact me. 
Might I suggest something? Maybe you could amend that SMS message advising customers to check their email - as opposed to sitting waiting for a text message that may never arrive?

Wanting to close an old WebSaver Plus account, my local branch advised me that I would have to speak to the team, and helpfully provided the phone number. reviews.title: Wonderful Staff - Shame About The Music!
reviews.body:
Wonderful Staff - Shame About The Music!
When I first applied for my new account, I was asked to 'phone because some of the information I had supplied apparently didn't match that held on your records. Evidently I had given the house name as well as the number on my new application and hadn't on the old one. Your very helpful staff updated the records but, by that time, my application had been rejected and I had to fill in a new online form. Bit of a hassle but I suppose these days security is paramount. Just a small point, your "hold" music is ghastly - sounds like a scratched gramaphone record (if you're old enough to remember what that's like!)

reviews.title: Very good service from Bearwood branch.
reviews.body:
Very good service from Bearwood branch.
It’s a bit of a mixed bag really. I wrote to WBBS with a query. I received a prompt written reply but the contents were a bit confusing. The letter asked me for details of my account but didn’t explain if that meant the account I had held with the building society or the count I currently hold with another provider. I had already explained that I could remember no details of the account I held with the WBBS. The account has not been used for at least 25 years!
I decided to go into my nearest branch in Bearwood near Birmingham. I received really good service there from a gentleman behind the counter, and especially from a lady who I assume to be the branch manager. I’m sorry I didn’t make a note of their names.

reviews.title: Exceptionally well informed member of staff
reviews.body:
Exceptionally well informed member of staff
I went into your branch in Sutton Coldfield,  West Midlands on Friday 27/06/2025 and was attended to by an extremely helpful, exceptionally efficient, knowledgeable member of staff. The lovely member of staff informed me that the ISA account (which I had deposited money into the previous day at another branch of The West Bromwich Buiding Society) was not in the best ISA account for me after i explained my circumstances. The member of staff went into great detail to explain everything perfectly clearly. I opened a new ISA with £18,000 (having invested £2,000 the previous day into an ISA acccount with  a much lower interest rate). My partner was also greatly impressed by this kind, polite, friendly member of staff that he opened an ISA with £20,000. My partner also put an additional £10,000 into another account. This is the only branch of The West Bromwich Buiding Society that I shall deal with in the future. 100% satisfaction.

reviews.title: Early exit fee with no good will gesture.
reviews.body:
Early exit fee with no good will gesture.
2 year mortgage product was 26 months. Sold house after 25 months of paying mortgage and West Brom wouldn’t reduce early exit fee of £5,400. Even as a good will to reduce by half. No movement and told we’ve signed for it so go away. Will never use again after customer service/complaints department have left me bitter and would rather use someone else. 

I know we had signed for it and weren’t expecting to move during the 2 year period but with the lack of care from the customer service I have told all family and friends to stay away and I will never use again.

Tried ringing their office on several occasions but could not get through, due to long wait times. Eventually got through and completed form. A week later received two identical letters posted separately informing me that my wife Catherine was now a MAN! Once again rang to try and get this changed, could not speak to anyone, eventually they rang me back and said they had checked the form I had submitted and suprise, suprise, I knew the sex of my wife and they admitted it was their fault and in the circumstances they would change the accounholders names. Not even an apology, but apparently, "we are very busy".

reviews.title: Possible scam alarm for no reason
reviews.body:
Possible scam alarm for no reason
I sent a postal application in respect of a 2 year fixed rate ISA (I have two other similar accounts).
I received a text message early on Thursday 12th May from the West Brom confirming receipt of that application.   However, a couple of ours later I received a further message indicating that my contact details had been changed and if this was not me I should ring back on a certain number.    Clearly this rang alarm bells as the details on the application were exactly the same as previous applications.  Had my application been intercepted and details changed I thought.  I checked the number and it was similar with the end digit different to the number I already had.
I rang this number expecting it to be dedicated to the query but it seemed like the normal switchboard, however, iI was encouraged (volume of calls etc.) to leave a number for a call back.  This happened fairly swiftly, but I was immediately asked for security info - as this was an incoming call to me I declined and rang off.   My husband ( who also has two ISAS accounts) thought this was the right thing to have done.   I was getting quite anxious at this time so my husband rang back through the main switchboard, first getting the web accounts team (Hannah) before being transferred to 'KP' to whom he explained the later text and need to check that contact details had not been changed.   Understandably he did not wish to discuss the issue with my husband until I had passed the security questions but these were more than just confirming personal details and not having  details of balances and account details other than 
the account numbers to hand I became quite distressed but eventually my husband got the file with the info and KP was able to discuss the matter with him.
The simple matter of confirming that our contact details had not been changed was then satisfactorily resolved.
It was not really explained why the text saying that they had was sent.
A lot of anxiousness caused for nothing!

reviews.title: Withdrawal of me ney for an emergency.
reviews.body:
Withdrawal of me ney for an emergency.
No problem using the internet link to view my accounts.  I have previously in branch been set up to transfer funds to a nominated bank account.  On the app it says it will send a code so you can proceed.  I received the code but there is no where to enter the code so ended up having to visit the branch to get cash as I needed it to pay for a car repair.  This is a safety issue.carrying a large amount of money especially as I was on the bus.  Your app needs to be worked on to ensure you can transfer money if it's an emergency.  No problem with the staff in branch they were very helpful but as I have to travel there on public transport this can be quite daunting.

reviews.title: Good products, staff … very difficult process, doubts on data security
reviews.body:
Good products, staff … very difficult process, doubts on data security
products are fine and call guy finally sorted out web access with was very helpful
BUT 
- problems started when only way to getPIN for web access was via call centre (we have many accounts and have never come across such a clunky difficult process before) 
- my wife did hers first … did work tho not happy with having to rely on call-back then  mega-input via phone pad etc etc 
- when I tried to follow with a call from different phone but same address/wifi IP I guess immediate auto message said along lines  ‘we have all info and will call you back if needed’ … tried calling several times, same effect so sent email asking what hell going on 
- next day a few calls from you, not convenient, phone was on don’t disturb … in fact I really dislike, am uncomfortable sorting things out on phone, expected and prefer email or secure messages 
- email from you received saying you tried calling requesting me to call 
- two days later i did call, very helpful guy put me on to auto PIN  after telling me what info I would need … hated doing it but it did work 
- didn’t have chance to question data from my wife apparently being used on my completely independent phone to block new call on grounds that presumably not needed
Very happy with service. Cashiers were very helpful.

reviews.title: A different style of application but thorough and efficient.
reviews.body:
A different style of application but thorough and efficient.
It is quite unusual nowadays to only be able to manage an account online rather than using a bank's own app, but the process of setting up access to the savings portal is efficient and the volume of security checks carried out during the process is reassuring. The timescale to assess an account application (3 working days) is longer than most I have encountered, but is clearly very thorough which also is reassuring. I was surprised the savings portal requires only a 4 digit pin in addition to account number and date of birth and would recommend something longer, at east 6 characters.

Excellent service from Chloe - she was friendly, listened to what I needed, explained answers clearly and did all she could to help. I could not fault her. Please pass on my thanks.

However, I had to call on this day after sending a query via email and receiving a call back the day before because my query had not been resolved. The person I spoke to wasn’t particularly friendly, didn’t deal with my query, didn’t empathise with what I was saying and spent the call explaining internal processes that are irrelevant to me as a customer. I would not have felt comfortable speaking to them further and would not trust information from them. If I call again and they answer, I’ll put the phone down.

Only rated 5 stars in case this review directly impacts Chloe, but service really depends on who answers the phone.

Efficient setup process and ease of use

My previous experience with West Brom was actually very good and I only moved my isa to another provider 2 months ago due to your reduction in rates. I applied for a savings account a couple of days ago and provided ID and 2 forms of address verification (fairly onerous compared to other regulated business' operating with the same legislation) The fist evidence  was a current council tax demand and the second a letter from yourselves addressed to me with my old account details, dated within the last 3 months. I was somewhat surprised therefore to be told my application had been declined because the address verification (your letter) was insufficient. A  bit of common sense would have told you that by referencing your letter addressed to me and showing my West Brom account number that you have already money laundered me. You need to make it easy for customers to do business with you and not work with a computer says no mentality. I have reapplied using a letter from a different financial institution, alongside my council tax bill. if you fail to open my account this time, then our relationship will be finished.

I could do in the West Bromwich branch what Lloyds Bank would not do in their branch. Which was open a cash ISA. West Bromwich got my business for this reason and the funds came from my other LLoyds accounts. Lloyds were internet or telephone only, not for me! Lloyds stated it was their poilcy to try and move customers onto the internet.
I do not expose my finances to the internet personally and hence sleep well at night ,with no concerns about security and scams!
Thank you West Bromwich Building Society for being there and making the process very easy!

reviews.body:
Extremely Disappointed

She sent him to the charity shop over the road. Not a pleasant experience and I won’t be Lee my money there as a direct result. We had travelled from the north west to Wednesbury to sort out some accounts. No empathy, no understanding, no case by case consideration. I have to say though, the other lady who actually served us was lovely, very helpful and understanding.

reviews.title: Need to register twice for online if you use WebSave & another WB product
reviews.body:
Need to register twice for online if you use WebSave & another WB product
My husband and I already had a joint account with them. Then we opened individul ISAs. We could not see them online. I emailed WBS and was told to ring a number. It turns out that the WBS has two parts - WebSave and Savings Portal. Though you may be registered for online access to one, you can not see the other. We both wasted a lot of time registering again for online access and it added to our phone bill though we were told that the calls (0345) were not charged.

reviews.title: Visit on the 17 April by *Personal information removed by Feefo*
reviews.body:
Visit on the 17 April by *Personal information removed by Feefo*
I attended before the Counter on 17 April and was met by a Asian female teller. She was rude and condensing. I had simply asked for an update on my tow books and had to transfer from one account to another. Instead, she was hyper critical that I had not set up the mandate from my current account. I had explained that I had not had the time to do so and furthermore she said that I had lost interest. Rather than provide a solution she berated me and I felt like just closing both accounts and taking my business elsewhere. She had no people skills and did not try and find a solution.

reviews.title: GREAT CUSTOMER SERVICE - SO MUCH BETTER THAN THE OTHERS
reviews.body:
GREAT CUSTOMER SERVICE - SO MUCH BETTER THAN THE OTHERS
I called to get an answer to a (in my mind) very mundane question, but, as usual, the answer was dealt with efficiently with great understanding, common sense and empathy.
I have always found the people dealing with "helpline" calls to be exceptionally well informed, friendly, empathic and , well.....just like you and I. The answers I received today were simple and straightforward, with (thankfully) no "Technospeak". As a consequence, I have no hesitation in calling The West Brom, in the certainty of getting an excellent response to my questions. Well done!

reviews.title: Poor attitude of customer service advisor
reviews.body:
Poor attitude of customer service advisor
As a relatively new home owner this was the first time I had ever had to call and speak to a customer service advisor and it was a terrible experience. The lady I spoke to was quite frankly rude, blunt and had a terrible attitude. I only wanted to change my payment date as I have changed jobs recently. The advisor gave me incorrect information, then had to put me on hold to confirm some details with the admin team, who confirmed I had been correct in the first instance. I hope all of your calls are recorded and mine is used for coaching the advisor I spoke with. Working in customer service myself I know I would have been spoken to by my own line manager had I dealt with a customer in the way she dealt with me. I will be shopping around at the end of my fixed term as I wouldn’t feel confident with the customer service you provide in the future if I ever had a significant issue.

reviews.title: Seeking Interest Figures for Tax Return.  Karen Most Helpful.
reviews.body:
Seeking Interest Figures for Tax Return.  Karen Most Helpful.
I spoke to Karen and she was great! Gave me the information that I needed very quickly (and confirmed quickly by post), was very helpful, but better than that - welcoming, friendly and I felt very safe in her hands. Like I was No1. This is just the sort of service that everybody should give, but often lacking in some institutions. I really don't think that Karen could improve on her performance and she is the tops. I have never had bad service from WBBS and I have been a customer since I first went to the Albion in 1977! Even though I am now at Everton I still like to keep my West Brom accounts and always will.

Was I was drawing a check from the West Brom to place elsewhere for liquidity purposes, there’s no sense of why are you taking your money away from here?

In short, should I be in a position to replace that withdrawal in a financial institution, the West Brom would definitely be on my short list of places to call.

reviews.title: Complications transferring funds from Websave to Savings portals.
reviews.body:
Complications transferring funds from Websave to Savings portals.
The people I spoke to were very helpful but I was less impressed with the complications of the system.   I was opening a new West Brom Double access ISA and funding it by transferring the balance from my Websave ISA.  I was amazed at the time it took.  2 days to close the websave ISA and a further day for the funds to appear in my Double access ISA.  Also, maybe I'm just plain thick, but I assumed West Brom Savings and your Websave savings to be the same organization and was somewhat amazed to be informed that they are two separate organizations.

reviews.title: Poor service, need to improve systems and procedures for online banking
reviews.body:
Poor service, need to improve systems and procedures for online banking
Very poor. If WBBS wants customers to go down the online banking route, it needs to sort it's systems and procedures out and move with the times. I have never had such a difficult time trying to open an account (the process takes several days) with a building society I have had accounts with since I was a child so known to them. I have now taken my £40k inheritance elsewhere and opened an online account within 30 minutes including completing all security checks.
My husband has also had a dreadful experience which is  
currently into day 4 although he's got further in the process than I have. He also has had an account with WBBS fir years but has had to jump through hoops to provide additional information to prove it is him. Bizarrely, they asked for a photograph of a cheque from his bank account elsewhere or a paying slip. How many banks issue chequebooks or paying in slips nowadays?! Having deposited £20k on 4/4/25, in advance of the ISA deadline, although WBBS acknowledged that lots of customers are having issues with their systems, they advised the money will not be put into the new ISA account until after the deadline.

reviews.title: It appears that the BS made an error
reviews.body:
It appears that the BS made an error
Website is not as clar as it needs to be: there needs to be crystal clear distinction/info on the site re which buttone to press when the customer is giving notice to close  notice acount with the notice period, and when the customer wants to close it immediately. 
Also: West Brom made a serious error in lsing my caccount. I pushed the wrong button (close immediately) then cancelled that instruction then requested closure with notice. I know i did it that way, and this is proven by hte text messages I recived (each instruction prompted an authentication code - the order of these is clear as i still have all the texts). However according to the BS I issued the cancellation instruction only after requesting closure with notie. This is incorrect. My account was closed without notice. I've been told that this did not incur any penalty - so I ahve not pursued it as if I've not lost interest it doesn't matter. Still, it's worrying that the West Brom got this wrong. I'm waiting for my statement and if I have lost interest I will pursue the error. 
Apart from this, interest rate is not great for a 60-day notice account!

reviews.title: Letter sent in error, poor internal access to IT system. Call too long.
reviews.body:
Letter sent in error, poor internal access to IT system. Call too long.
As one of two powers of attorney, I contacted them because we'd received a letter saying that they were acknowledging the change of address . This was incorrect so I contacted them on the customer service phone number given in the letter to confirm the details had and what was the reason for the letter to be sent out.
They had two addresses for my relative.  (1) A care home and (2) a postal  contact address.
The letter had my name (despite the other power of attorney being allocated as the main contact person ) and my relatives old home address (3) which has since been sold, both of which were incorrect.

 Customer service couldn't find the sent letter on their system, even though I gave the date and ref no. (Neither the person on the phone or her boss that she went to see)

They contacted another dept, the one that sends letters out. (Customer service were not anymore access their system to locate the letter). 

The second dept said the letter was sent to me at address (3) by system error, and it has been confirmed to me that they have now removed that address from the system completely.
It has also been confirmed categorically that all communication will go to address (2) and be addressed to the other power of attorney. 

It took 3 hours and 50 mins to resolve what it's a simple issue, message more complicated very the letter itself. Considering the letter said to contact customer services about the change of address, it's crazy that they can't access the information and it is held with another department.

They need to sort their computer system and customer communication out so it works more efficiently.

reviews.title: Poor account opening processes for existing customers
reviews.body:
Poor account opening processes for existing customers
My new savings application was turned down because my personal details were supposedly different to what West Brom held and the email said I should check my details. When I logged on my personal details couldn't be accessed. So I rang up but was cut off mid-call. Rang up a  second time, answered fairly quickly, but it turned out everything was identical except the address they held contained my county but my application didn't have the county on it. Postcode, house number, street, town etc all the same. Despite this I was told they still couldn't process my application and I would have to apply again. Not impressed with this and closed my account instead.

reviews.title: Online only products are not the way forward if you want happy customers
reviews.body:
Online only products are not the way forward if you want happy customers
This is a score relating to the process of buying an ISA, compared to an ISA purchase I made with the West From 18months ago. I wanted to purchase another ISA as part of my annual allowance, but the only way to do it was online. The process was not particularly easy, consequently I had to make two phone calls to the Bsocto clarify parts of the process. All in all it took several hours and a wait for the approval process to be completed, then further time spent trying to transfer money in. This was way more complicated, stressful and time consuming than going into a branch and having a friendly human process it for you. On top of that, there is no physical certificate, it all has to be viewed and managed online going forward. This is not progress for the customer and it takes the human service element away. How ironic then that all banks and BSoc's spend millions on marketing and PR campaigns trying to convince us they are "there" for us. Rubbish!

reviews.title: WB error at opening  needed correction which was tardy
reviews.body:
WB error at opening  needed correction which was tardy
When opening each of two new accounts online, the first question was do I have a WB account and I answered YES. The second was do I have a Web Save account and I answered YES and gave my ACCOUNT NUMBER. The accounts opened and I received e mail confirmation and deposited a trial £1  to the Four Access and £10 to the Reg Saver then went to look on my online portal and found that the new Reg Saver was visible but not the Four Access.
When I phoned on Monday  to ask what was happening, the WB employee told me that their" administrator" had registered me as a new profile for the Four Access but used my existing profile for the Reg Save. They said that merging only happens on Tuesdays and that I would have to wait until Friday to be able to see the Four Access online.
Good that the guy was able to sort it when I phoned but bad mistake in the first place and poor to not be able to correct immediately.

Customers should be able to see all of their accounts online under one login, instead of separate logins for Websave and other WestBrom savings.  They should also have the ability to shift funds directly from Websave into non-Websave WestBrom accounts, and vice versa.
Setting up a new account online is clunky.  I got an email telling me that although it was set up (after several days of waiting, even though I was an existing customer), I needed to register a PIN by phone in order to access it online.  This went through automated menus, and then a real person, and then another  automated process, before I finally got a text saying that I could now use the PIN for telephone access.  No mention of online access, and no email providing a link to the login page or any other information.  Nor did the assistant mention how to log in online.  If you've set up the account online, it stands to reason that you'll want to access it online, surely?

reviews.title: Lied to by advisor
reviews.body:
Lied to by advisor
My experience with West Brom has been bad during my remortgage and move to another provider. My solicitor contacted West Brom a number of times to request a redemption statement. We were then told that you West Brom could not receive emails or calls from this company. This has been resulted in a delay to our remortgage. One advisor I spoke to me lied about my statement being sent and sent me on a wild goose chase with my solicitor to find the redemption statement, even providing me with a false date and the time I was sent. This was incredibly poor service, the second advisor I spoke to did rectify the issue and provided much better service. However, there was no explanation as I to why I was lied to. This was incredibly frustrating and I wouldn’t recommend West Brom based on this. I will raise a complaint in regard to this matter.

reviews.title: Helpful overall
reviews.body:
Helpful overall
The account I have had for some years has been fine. However I had received the email advising me of the lower interest rate and offering a better rate account within the company. I appreciate this information being given, as with all banks and building societies. It came as a surprise however to know that Websave and West Brom function independently and I thought it none too straightforward to switch to a different account. The telephone helper did a good job at explaining this nevertheless.
The application process required knowing what my annual income is and as  I did not have this figure immediately to hand. It therefore took me quite some time to gather this info (suggested time for the application was 10 mins!)
I was almost at the end of the application when I was asked how much I was 'looking to invest' in the account. I wanted to switch the sum of my existing account into the new account but had no info about how and when to transfer the money.
 It was then explained on the phone that this was just a marketing question and I was not bound by the figure I entered. i didn't feel this was made at all clear on the form. He offered to transfer me to the West Brom handler who was able to explain that I could complete my application but pay my money in within 30 days.
I was relieved to receive an immediate acknowledgement of my application by text with the news that I would be contacted within the next few days. However the application process all took far long in my opinion. Call handlers were extremely polite, helpful and friendly but I feel the system lets them down.

reviews.title: Great service, poor systems
reviews.body:
Great service, poor systems
Great service and I'm pleased with the remortgage.
West Brom need to invest in updating their systems- which are not able to cope simultaneously with increasing loan amount, term and change to a new 5 year fix at a future point in time So all the calculations reflected only part of this and so were incorrect. 
Secondly, we were then rung by a separate West Brom team about remortgage options after everything was agreed. This highlights the need for one customer database and system.

reviews.title: Happy People
reviews.body:
Happy People
I live near on 90 miles away from any local West Brom branch, so I cannot comment on any particular bank of theirs, but the few interactions I have had with their staff I found pleasant friendly helpful people, which is a major bonus over all the digital robots we have to endure with most banks and building society's nowadays.
I think their website portal could do with an overhaul and update to appear more modern, with a mobile app to complement that upgrade.
I have used 3 different West Brom phone numbers, 1 centralised number or all departments listed clearly on the front page would be nice.
Looking at my mortgage statement online I can only see 1 total amount and interest rate percentage, I would much prefer to see a statement of each payment accumulation alongside that.
Overall I've had nil problems with the West Brom and I'm thankful for their mortgage.

reviews.title: Lacks basic application of security procedures regarding identity evidence
reviews.body:
Lacks basic application of security procedures regarding identity evidence
WBBS has asked for evidence of identity be supplied multiple times in an insecure format (email). No direct upload was possible - timed out before information could be obtained and supplied, since only three days allowed. Supplied the information they requested by email as requested despite reservations and I am told that the attachment to the email was not there. It most definitely was, so they are either lying, or their system strips attachments from external emails.
